channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Fly-tipping issues from same person, 16th March
item.description:
I would like to report repeated fly-tipping by the same individual. This is not a one-off incident. The waste is being dumped regularly by a specific neighbour. I have evidence showing that the same person is responsible. The fly-tipping consists of household waste being left in the street / communal area rather than being disposed of properly. This has happened multiple times and is creating an ongoing problem. I have photos/videos that show the person responsible and the waste being left at the location. These can be provided as evidence if required. Details: • Location: 17 Walworth Place SE17 2GL Halcyon House • Dates observed: 16/03/2026 • Type of waste: household rubbish Because this behaviour is repeated and the person responsible can be identified, I am requesting that the matter be investigated by Environmental Enforcement. Please let me know if further information or evidence is required.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 293, Walworth Place, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish everywhere, 16th March
item.description:
The pavement outside the shops on the Sidcup Road at Fiveways are covered in litter and the bin there is full. It’s looking particularly untidy and gives a poor impression of the area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Felhampton Road, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Child’s car seat on grass verge that is between Finchale Road and Cookhill Road., 16th March
item.description:
Car seat is on the edge of the grass verge near to the pavement that leads to Cookhill Road in the direction of the school. The seat is in two pieces.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Finchale Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Smashed car window glass, 16th March
item.description:
There is lots of smashed glass strewn across the pavement and road, this should be cleared up as this is a residential area with lots of families and young children play on the green just by this.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hillcrest Road, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bike, 16th March
item.description:
Pedal cycle has been abandoned for over 1 year outside Dr surgery

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ceres Road, Plumstead, Greater London

item.title: Dumped rubbish, 16th March
item.description:
Rubbish left in front of a vacant area, this rubbish is being blown up and down the street. A bit further along there is smashed glass from a broken window, a separate report will be created

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hillcrest Road, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Furniture dump, 16th March
item.description:
As per image

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sanford Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bins ready to overflow AGAIN., 16th March
item.description:
Ongoing problem with these waste bins. They are now full up again, so people will start just dumping carrier bags and other rubbish on top of the bins or on the floor! It looks unsightly and scruffy. Cant the bins just be removed to stop people using them for their household rubbish and the local scrotes from chucking their empty beer cans in them! Sick of seeing this every day.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hazel Walk, Southborough, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned bike, 16th March
item.description:
Stripped frame, evidence of damage - has been there several days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Garnham Close, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old chair and bags of rubbish dumped, 16th March
item.description:
As pre picture - this is an ongoing fly tipping spot

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sanford Walk, Hackney, London

item.title: Rubbish strewn along walkway, 16th March
item.description:
As per image plus a discarded air fryer - area previously reported for same issue

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Sanford Walk, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chippings and large pothole., 16th March
item.description:
This keeps going on report as duplicate, but it has got worse and not yet white marked for repair. Re chippings, a team cleaned the whole junction last week, but missed the covered yellow lines as shown in picture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Austin Road, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Repeated dog fouling on Brunel museum premises, 16th March

Repeated dog fouling on Brunel museum premises

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tunnel Road, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Uneven and cracked pavements along Sundridge Avenue, 16th March
item.description:
These pavements have been cracked for months and getting worse - my children have tripped on them on multiple occasions. Please fix asap.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sundridge Avenue, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumped by owners, 16th March
item.description:
Number 1 Northumberland park Road Erith, they are having work done at end of garden which backs onto the alleyway that runs behind this road and Horsa Road Erith, they have removed the increasing amount of rubbish from their garden and dumped it in the alleyway behind their property, apparently this may be a hmo as tenants there, so many of us residents in Horsa Road have to continually put up with fly tipping in the alleyway which you have said you will not help which I find disgusting but we cannot get access to our garage to park our car PLEASE investigate this ongoing issue and speak to Number 1 Northumberland park Road this needs to be done urgently

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 198-200, Northumberland Park, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road failing, 16th March
item.description:
Poor road surface on junction Eastern Way/ Nathan Way
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Nathan Way, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unnecessary lane closure creating traffic, 16th March
item.description:
The eastbound junction of Bostall Hill and Knee Hill - at the traffic lights - has been reduced to one lane with a quick light changing sequence, causing massive traffic queues. This is due to unnecessary closure of the lane which is for straight and right, causing all traffic going ahead and left to compete for one lane. It is clearly visible that Brampton road is closed and nothing beyond the No right turn sign is necessary, as the closed lane is not exclusively a Turn right only lane. This poor road management was previously in place for weeks, causing big issues for traffic and is now back in the same format.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woolwich Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
